response.justification: Strong distributed-systems background (sub-millisecond micro-services at Sociomantic, AWS-based platforms at Tencent) and current Staff-level work at Naver on MCP/agentic tooling aligns well with the AI-native engineering requirement. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the stated skills, with the candidate's strengths concentrated in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Some resume entries (e.g., 'Founder, MercadoLibre' overlapping multiple full-time roles) are ambiguous and warrant clarification in an interview.
strengths: Current Staff role with direct hands-on MCP/agentic-workflow tooling experience
Proven high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of req/s at Sociomantic)
Deep software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, workshops/teaching
concerns: Python not listed among skills despite being a must-have
No explicit on-call/incident-response or stakeholder-management framing in resume
Ambiguous/overlapping employment entries (e.g., 'Founder, MercadoLibre' alongside full-time roles) need clarification

key_factors.factor: Agentic/MCP tooling experience at current role
